title: React Grab Codebase Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the React Grab monorepo - a tool that lets you select context for coding agents directly from your website

steps.title: Welcome to React Grab
steps.description: React Grab is a monorepo that lets developers point at any UI element and press Cmd+C to copy the file name, React component, and HTML source code - making coding agents like Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot run up to 3x faster. The project uses pnpm workspaces with Turborepo for build orchestration across 20+ packages.

steps.title: Core Library - packages/react-grab
steps.description: The heart of the project. This package contains the SolidJS-based overlay UI, element selection logic, clipboard handling, keyboard shortcuts, and the plugin system. Key areas: src/core/ handles selection, events, and copy logic; src/components/ renders the overlay, context menu, toolbar, and selection labels; src/utils/ contains focused helper functions.

steps.title: Core Internals - Selection & Plugins
steps.description: The core/ directory is where element selection, keyboard handling, clipboard operations, and the plugin registry live. The agent/ subdirectory manages AI agent sessions. Built-in plugins (copy, open, comment, copy-html, copy-styles) extend functionality via the plugin-registry system. The store and events modules manage reactive state using SolidJS signals.

steps.title: CLI - Developer Setup Tool
steps.description: The packages/cli package powers 'npx grab@latest init' - the one-command setup experience. It detects your framework (Next.js, Vite, Webpack), installs react-grab, and injects the correct script tag or import. Commands include init, add (for MCP integration), remove, and configure. The utils/ directory handles framework detection, code transforms, and MCP server installation.

steps.title: Agent Provider Packages
steps.description: React Grab supports 9 coding agents, each with a dedicated provider package: Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Codex, Gemini, Amp, Droid, OpenCode, and Ami. Each provider follows a consistent pattern with client.ts (browser-side relay client), server.ts (WebSocket/SSE server), handler.ts (agent-specific message handling), and cli.ts (registration entry point).

steps.title: Relay & MCP - Communication Layer
steps.description: The relay package provides the WebSocket-based communication bridge between the browser and coding agents. It defines the protocol, manages connections, and handles client/server messaging. The mcp package exposes React Grab as a Model Context Protocol server, letting agents query element context programmatically.

steps.title: Browser Extension
steps.description: The web-extension package bundles React Grab as a Chrome extension so it works on any website without code changes. It uses a service worker for background tasks, a content script bridge to inject React Grab into pages, and follows the Manifest V3 extension architecture.

steps.title: Monorepo Infrastructure
steps.description: React Grab uses pnpm workspaces with Turborepo for task orchestration. Changesets handle versioning and publishing. Key config files: turbo.json defines the build pipeline, pnpm-workspace.yaml declares workspace packages, vercel.json configures deployment, and AGENTS.md provides coding conventions (SolidJS reactivity rules, kebab-case files, arrow functions, no type casting).
